Citizenship of Zambia Regulations, 2017 (Statutory Instrument 50 of 2017) Zambia
Zambia
Citizenship of Zambia Act, 2016
Citizenship of Zambia Regulations, 2017
Statutory Instrument 50 of 2017
Published in Government Gazette on 30 June 2017
Assented to on 28 June 2017
Commenced on 30 June 2017
[This is the version of this document as it was from 1 January 2022 to 30 December 2025.]
[Amended by Citizenship of Zambia (Amendment) Regulations,
2022 (Statutory Instrument 27 of 2022) on 1 January 2022]
IN EXERCISE of the powers contained in section 45 of the Citizenship of Zambia Act, 2016, the following
Regulations are made:

sections.heading: Title
sections.text:
1. Title
These Regulations may be cited as the Citizenship of Zambia Regulations, 2017.

sections.heading: Interpretation
Notice of intention to apply for citizenship by registration
sections.text:
3. Notice of intention to apply for citizenship by registration
A person who intends to apply for citizenship by registration under Article 37 of the Constitution shall
publish in the Gazette and in two consecutive issues of a daily newspaper circulating in the person’s area of
residence, a notice of the person’s intention in Form I set out in the First Schedule.
[Cap. 1]

Citizenship of Zambia Regulations, 2017 (Statutory Instrument 50 of 2017) Zambia

sections.heading: Application for citizenship by registration
sections.text:
4. Application for citizenship by registration
A person who has complied with regulation 3 shall apply to the Board for citizenship by registration in
Form II set out in the First Schedule upon payment of the application fee specified in the Second Schedule.

sections.heading: Request for additional information
sections.text:
5. Request for additional information
The Board may, in relation to an application for citizenship by registration, request an applicant to submit
additional information in Form III set out in the First Schedule.

sections.heading: Grant or rejection of application for citizenship by registration
sections.text:
6. Grant or rejection of application for citizenship by registration
(1) The Board shall, within six months of the receipt of an application for citizenship by registration,
grant or reject the application.
(2) The Board shall reject an application for citizenship by registration that does not meet the
requirements of the Constitution and the Act.
(3) Where the Board rejects an application for citizenship, the Board shall inform the applicant, in
writing, of the rejection and the reasons therefor, within seven days of the rejection.
(4) The Board shall, where an application for citizenship by registration is granted, register the
applicant in accordance with section 23 of the Act.
[Cap. 1]

sections.heading: Certificate of citizenship by registration
sections.text:
7. Certificate of citizenship by registration
The Board shall, upon registration of an applicant in accordance with regulation 6(4), issue the applicant
with a certificate of citizenship by registration in Form IV set out in the First Schedule.

sections.heading: Notice of citizenship by adoption
sections.text:
8. Notice of citizenship by adoption
(1) A citizen who is the adoptive parent or legal guardian of a child who, before being adopted, was not
a citizen, shall within thirty days of the adoption notify the Board of that child’s becoming a citizen
by adoption in Form V set out in the First schedule.
(2) The Board shall register an adopted child notified in terms of subregulation (1) as a citizen within
twenty-one working days of receipt of the notification.

sections.heading: Notice of acquisition of citizenship of another country
sections.text:
9. Notice of acquisition of citizenship of another country
(1) A citizen who acquires the citizenship of another country shall notify the Board of the acquisition of
that citizenship in Form VI set out in the First Schedule.
(2) The Board shall cause to be entered in the register of citizens who hold dual citizenship, the names
of a citizen who acquires the citizenship of another country.

sections.heading: Bestowal of citizenship
sections.text:
10. Bestowal of citizenship
(1) A person who ceased to be a citizen as a result of acquiring the citizenship of another country may
apply to the Board for bestowal of the citizenship in Form VII set out in the First Schedule.
(2) An applicant for bestowal of citizenship under this regulation may, where the applicant is abroad,
lodge the application for bestowal of citizenship with the Zambian mission in the country of that
applicant’s residence or where there is no Zambian mission in that country, in the nearest country
where there is a Zambian mission.
By Laws.Africa and contributors. Licensed under CC-BY. Share widely and freely. 2
Citizenship of Zambia Regulations, 2017 (Statutory Instrument 50 of 2017) Zambia
(3) The Board shall cause to be entered in the register of citizens who hold dual citizenship or another
appropriate register, the names of a person on whom citizenship is bestowed under this regulation.
(4) The Board shall issue an applicant under this regulation with a certificate of bestowal of citizenship
in Form VIII set out in the First Schedule.

sections.heading: Oath of allegiance
sections.text:
11. Oath of allegiance
A person who is granted citizenship by registration shall take an oath of allegiance in Form IX set out in
the First Schedule and lodge the oath with the citizenship office.

sections.heading: Renunciation of citizenship or citizenship of other country
sections.text:
12. Renunciation of citizenship or citizenship of other country
(1) A citizen may renounce that citizen’s citizenship by making a declaration in Form X set out in the
First Schedule and lodge the declaration with the citizenship office.
(2) The Board shall issue a citizen who has renounced citizenship with a certificate of renunciation in
Form XI set out in the First Schedule.
(3) A person shall, upon being issued with a certificate of renunciation in accordance with
subregulation (2), surrender to the citizenship office that person’s national registration card or
other national identity document for cancellation.
(4) A citizen who has renounced that citizen’s citizenship of another country shall notify the Board
of the renunciation in writing and attach a certified copy of the certificate of renunciation or
equivalent document issued by that country in respect of the renunciation.
(5) A citizen referred to in subregulation (4) shall give the notice required under that subregulation—
(a) where the citizen is in Zambia, within fourteen days of the renunciation; or
(b) where the citizen is abroad, within six months of the renunciation.
(6) The Board shall cause to be removed from, and entered in, the appropriate register the names of
a citizen who renounces that citizen’s citizenship or citizenship of another country under this
regulation.

sections.heading: Deprivation of citizenship
sections.text:
13. Deprivation of citizenship
(1) The Board shall, where the Board intends to deprive a citizen of citizenship, give notice of the
intention to that citizen in Form XII set out in the First Schedule.
(2) The notice referred to in subregulation (1) shall, where the location of the citizen to be notified is—
(a) known, be delivered to that citizen personally or sent to that citizen by registered post; or
(b) not known, sent to that citizen’s last known address.
(3) A citizen notified of the Board’s intention to deprive that citizen of citizenship may apply to the
Board, in writing for an inquiry provided for in section 28 of the Act—
(a) if the citizen is in Zambia at the time of being notified, within seven days from the date of
receipt of the notice; or
(b) if the citizen is outside Zambia, within fourteen days from the date of receipt of the notice.
(4) Despite subregulation (3), the Board may in special circumstances extend the time within which an
application for an inquiry provided for in section 28 of the Act may be made.
(5) The Board shall, where the Board deprives a citizen of citizenship, give notice of deprivation to the
citizen in Form XIII set out in the First Schedule.
By Laws.Africa and contributors. Licensed under CC-BY. Share widely and freely. 3
Citizenship of Zambia Regulations, 2017 (Statutory Instrument 50 of 2017) Zambia
(6) The Board shall cause the name of a citizen deprived of citizenship to be removed from the
applicable register of citizens and entered in the register of persons who have renounced or been
deprived of citizenship.
(7) A person deprived of citizenship or any other person in possession of the national registration card
or other national identity document belonging to a person deprived of citizenship shall, as soon as
is practicable, deliver the card to the citizenship office for cancellation.

sections.heading: Publication of names of persons who cease to be citizens
sections.text:
14. Publication of names of persons who cease to be citizens
The Board shall, as soon as is practicable after a person has ceased to be a citizen, publish a notice of the
cessation of that person’s citizenship in the Gazette.

sections.heading: Form of registers
Certification of documents
sections.text:
16. Certification of documents
A passports and citizenship officer may, for purposes of section 44(2) of the Act, certify a document to be
a true copy of the original by a written statement to that effect signed by that passports and citizenship
officer.

sections.heading: Detention of certain documents
sections.text:
17. Detention of certain documents
A law enforcement officer may detain a national registration card or other national identity document for
a period of not more than twenty-eight days for the purposes of conducting an investigation where the law
enforcement officer has reasonable grounds to believe that the national registration card or other national
identity document—
(a) was obtained wrongfully or fraudulently; or
(b) is wrongfully or fraudulently in the possession of a person.

sections.heading: Letter of confirmation
sections.text:
18. Letter of confirmation
A holder of a permit issued under the Immigration and Deportation Act, 2010, may apply to the Board, in
writing, for a letter confirming that the person has not applied for citizenship.
[Act No. 18 of 2010]

sections.heading: Prescribed fees
sections.text:
19. Prescribed fees
The fees set out in the Second Schedule are prescribed in respect of the matters specified in that Schedule.

sections.heading: Revocation of SI No. 56 of 1977
sections.text:
20. Revocation of SI No. 56 of 1977
The Citizenship of Zambia Regulations, 1977, are revoked.
